activity.body: Karen Chen, Discharge Planner at UCSF Mission Bay — booked intro for Thu 2:30 PM.

messages.body:
Hi Karen,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run a home-care agency in SF called Family Care SF. We're family-owned, been around since 2001, and we send caregivers into people's homes for things like personal care, companionship, fall-risk help, and overnights.

I'm trying to introduce ourselves to a few discharge teams in the city this year. We tend to do well on cases that need overnights, weekend coverage, or a Russian, Tagalog, or Spanish-speaking caregiver — that's just where we're a little deeper than most.

Not looking for a meeting. Just wanted you to know we exist for the next time one of those cases needs a hand.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Hi Alex — that'd actually be useful. Weekend discharges fall through on us too often. Languages and overnight coverage matter. Thursday afternoon work for a quick call?

Thursday's good. We've got caregivers in English, Russian, Tagalog, and Spanish — overnights and weekends are kind of our thing. 2:30 work for you?

Alex
Confirmed — Thu May 14, 2:30 PM. Calendar invite sent.

activity.body: David Reyes, Elder Law at Reyes & Goldman — "send me your intake packet, I have two clients this week."

messages.body:
Hi David,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run a small family home-care agency in SF (Family Care SF, on Ocean Ave). We've been at this since 2001. Mostly companion care, personal care, overnights — caregivers in someone's home, not nursing.

I know elder law attorneys don't really need another vendor pitch in their inbox, so I'll keep this short. A few fiduciaries I've worked with have said the boring stuff is what's hard to find — caregivers who show up on time, leave clean notes, and call you when something changes instead of when there's already a problem. That's most of what we try to do.

If you ever have a conservatorship or trust client who needs help started at home, happy to be one of the names you call.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Send me your intake packet — I have two clients this week. One in Pacific Heights, one in Forest Hill. Both private pay, both need reliable starts.

activity.body: Priya Anand, Social Worker at Laguna Honda — asked about overnight rates and minimum hours.

messages.body:
Hi Priya,

Quick intro — I'm Alex Girsh and I run a family home-care agency here in SF called Family Care SF. We send caregivers into people's homes for personal care, companionship, overnights, that kind of thing. Been around since 2001.

The one thing worth mentioning: we have a number of Russian, Tagalog, and Spanish-speaking caregivers on staff. If a Laguna Honda discharge ever needs a language match and your usual list is thin, we're happy to be a backup name. We'll get back to you fast either way.

That's all — wanted to make sure you know we're out here.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Thanks Alex. Two quick questions: what's the overnight rate, and what's your minimum hours per shift? We have a Russian-speaking discharge tomorrow if you can staff it.

activity.body: Dr. Marcus Hall, Geriatrician at One Medical Pacific Heights — wants to add us to his referral list.

thread.subject: If you keep a home-care list

messages.body:
Hi Dr. Hall,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run a small home-care agency here in SF called Family Care SF. We've been at it 24 years. What we do is send caregivers into older folks' homes — personal care, companion, fall-risk help, overnights. Not nursing, not medical.

I know you probably get a lot of emails like this, so I'll be short. If you keep a short list of home-care agencies for patients who need help at home, I'd be grateful to be on it. We pick up the phone, we answer fast, and we won't say yes to a case unless we can actually staff it.

That's it. Thanks for reading.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Alex — yes, please add us to your one-pager list and I'll add you to mine. I refer about 3-5 a month. Do you cover Pacific Heights / Presidio Heights weekend overnights?

activity.body: Linda Okafor, Director at Rhoda Goldman Plaza — "not a fit right now, check back in Q3."

messages.body:
Hi Linda,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run a small family home-care agency here in SF called Family Care SF. Been around since 2001. We send caregivers into people's homes — companion, personal care, fall-risk, overnights.

Just wanted to introduce ourselves in case one of your residents ever needs more support than IL can give and you keep a list of outside agencies. Happy to be a backup name. Not looking for a meeting.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Hi Alex — appreciate the note. Not a fit right now; we have a preferred list locked in through Q2. Check back in Q3 and I'll take a real look.

activity.body: James Whitcombe, Admissions at Sequoias SF — 30-min call confirmed Mon 10:00 AM.

messages.body:
Hi James,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run a home-care agency in SF called Family Care SF, family-owned, around since 2001. We send caregivers into people's homes — companion, personal care, overnights, fall-risk help. Not nursing, not medical.

Wanted to introduce ourselves to a few senior-living teams in the city. If your residents ever need more help than IL covers and you keep a short list of outside agencies, I'd love to be on it. Happy to drop off a one-pager if that's easier than another email.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Hi Alex — yes, worth a quick chat. Mon 10 AM PT works for me. 30 minutes. I'll send a Zoom link.

activity.body: Outreach batch sent — 52 contacts across hospitals, attorneys, and senior living.
Rachel Brodsky, Care Manager at Institute on Aging — open to a partnership conversation.

messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run a small home-care agency here in SF called Family Care SF (we're family-owned, been around since 2001). We send caregivers into homes — personal care, companion, overnights, that kind of thing.

I know IOA does a lot of care management already, so I'm not pitching you anything fancy. If you ever have a plan that needs reliable hands at home and your usual staffing partner is stretched, we'd be happy to help. We pick up the phone, answer fast, and won't say yes if we can't actually do it.

That's it — just wanted to put us on your radar.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Alex — open to a partnership conversation. We have plans that drift because the staffing partner isn't responsive. If you're consistent on communication and weekend coverage, we should talk.

lastWeek.recommendation: Prioritize the 3 warm leads above this week — same-week follow-up tends to convert ~2× better than waiting.
